pi_items.source: Task description line 6: 'Save the drawing as a DXF file to '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/compass_rose.dxf''
Task description line 1: 'Create a new layer named 'COMPASS_ROSE' and place ALL entities on it'
Task description line 2: 'Draw an outer circle centered at (0,0) with radius 100'
Task description line 3: 'Draw an inner circle centered at (0,0) with radius 20'
Task description line 4: 'Draw 4 cardinal direction lines starting from (0,0) and extending 100 units outward (North to 0,100; East to 100,0; South to 0,-100; West to -100,0)'
Task description line 5: 'Draw 4 ordinal direction lines (NE, NW, SE, SW) starting from (0,0) and extending 70 units outward at 45-degree angles'

Verifier code (verifier.py line 46): tolerance used for geometric verification. Value represents ±3 units of acceptable deviation for point positions and radii. Approximately 3% tolerance relative to outer radius of 100 units, consistent with engineering drawing standards (1-5% typical tolerance range).

privileged_info_summary: The compass_rose_symbol task requires creating a synthetic 8-point compass rose drawing in LibreCAD. All metadata values are verified as correct: outer circle radius 100 units, inner circle radius 20 units, cardinal direction lines extending 100 units in cardinal directions (North, South, East, West), ordinal direction lines extending 70 units at 45-degree angles, placed on a layer named 'COMPASS_ROSE', saved to /home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/compass_rose.dxf. The geometric tolerance of 3.0 units is used for verification and represents approximately 3% of the maximum feature dimension, which is reasonable and consistent with engineering standards.
